weatherForecast.comment: During the night, clouds will gradually gather and spread in the morning hours. On Wednesday, visibility will be limited at times, especially in the Hohe Tauern and in the high altitudes of the northern Alps. From the Kitzbühel Alps to the Osterhorn group, light rain showers are possible in the morning, with unproductive snow showers above 2000 metres. Pongau and Lungau will be mostly dry. From midday it will be dry everywhere again and the sun will appear at times. Spring clouds will form throughout the mountains, with isolated light showers possible in the Tauern mountains in the afternoon. The wind will blow weakly from the west. At 2000 metres around 0 degrees, at 3000 metres around -7 or -6 degrees.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak persistent weak layer and wet snow
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is moderate. In a few places, especially in the aspects from west to north to east, medium and occasionally large dry slab avalanches can be triggered by individuals. Danger areas are difficult to recognise. Take particular care at the transitions from little to more snow.

Occasional wet snow avalanches triggered by individuals are possible on sunny slopes, usually early in the morning, but at the latest as the snowpack softens during daytime changes. These usually remain medium-sized at most. The likelihood of gliding avalanches is somewhat increased.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack is only able to radiate to a very limited extent in some areas and there is hardly any snow crust capable of bearing loads, especially towards the main Alpine ridge. In shady high altitudes, soft, faceted snow can often still be found on the surface. Below this lies a mostly well-set sequence of compact drift snow layers. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor. The deep and sunny middle layers are largely snowed out.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in the avalanche danger despite the cold front on Thursday.

weatherForecast.comment: During the night, clouds will gradually gather and spread in the morning hours. On Wednesday, visibility will be limited at times, especially in the Hohe Tauern and in the high altitudes of the northern Alps. From the Kitzbühel Alps to the Osterhorn group, light rain showers are possible in the morning, with unproductive snow showers above 2000 metres. Pongau and Lungau will be mostly dry. From midday it will be dry everywhere again and the sun will appear at times. Spring clouds will form throughout the mountains, with isolated light showers possible in the Tauern mountains in the afternoon. The wind will blow weakly from the west. At 2000 metres around 0 degrees, at 3000 metres around -7 or -6 degrees.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is moderate from around 2000 metres and low below that. In a few places, especially in the aspects from west to north to east, medium and in exceptional cases even large slab avalanches can be triggered by individuals. Danger areas are difficult to recognise. Take particular care at the transitions from little to more snow.

Wet snow avalanches are possible on the sunny slopes as the snowpack softens. These usually remain small, but can break through into weak layers close to the ground at high altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack can cool down overnight and form at least a thin melt-freeze crust. On sunny slopes, this softens during the day and the snowpack is damp or wet up to high altitudes. In shady high altitudes, soft, faceted snow can often still be found on the surface. Below this lies a predominantly well-set sequence of compact drift snow layers. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor. The deep and sunny middle layers are largely snowed out.

tendency.highlights: On Wednesday, a slight deterioration in the weather with spring clouds and unproductive precipitation.

weatherForecast.comment: During the night, clouds will gradually gather and spread in the morning hours. On Wednesday, light rain showers are possible in the north in the morning. From midday, it will be dry everywhere again and the sun will appear at times. Spring clouds will form throughout the mountains. The wind will be light from westerly directions. Around 0 degrees at 2000 metres.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low risk of isolated wet snow avalanches
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is low. Occasional small wet snow avalanches are possible on the few sunny slopes that have not yet been discharged.

In addition, small snow slabs can still be triggered in exceptional cases in the entrance areas of steep gullies on shady slopes with large additional loads. The risk of being swept away and falling on icy surfaces generally outweighs the risk of burial.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack is moist to wet and well settled right up to the summit areas. Only a thin melt-freeze crust forms overnight. In the higher, on shady slopes, there are still weak layers of large faceted crystals in the lower part of the snowpack, especially close to the ground. Deep and sunny middle layers are covered with snow.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in the avalanche danger despite the cold front on Thursday.

weatherForecast.comment: During the night, clouds will gradually gather and spread in the morning hours. On Wednesday, visibility will be limited at times, especially at high altitudes in the northern Alps. In the Kitzbühel Alps, light rain showers are possible in the morning, with unproductive snow showers above 2000 metres. The Pongau and Lungau will be mostly dry. From midday it will be dry everywhere again and the sun will appear at times. Spring clouds will form throughout the mountains, with isolated light showers possible in the Tauern mountains in the afternoon. The wind will be light from the west. At 2000 metres it will be around 0 degrees.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is low. Medium-sized slab avalanches can still be triggered in a few hard-to-recognise places, especially in the extended northern sector above 1800 m, but usually only with a large additional load. This is in particular at the transitions from little to more snow.

On the sunny slopes, isolated wet snow avalanches are possible as the snowpack softens. These usually remain small.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack can cool down overnight and form at least a thin melt-freeze crust. On sunny slopes, this softens during the day and the snowpack is damp or wet up to high altitudes. In shady high altitudes, some soft, faceted snow can still be found on the surface, some of which is already crusted. Below this lies a mostly well-set sequence of compact drift snow layers. The connection at the transition to the old snow foundation, which consists mainly of deep rime, is still poor. The deep and sunny middle layers are largely snowed out.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in the avalanche danger despite the cold front on Thursday.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Persistent weak layer continues to allow large avalanches
